display.label: Syphilis — Antenatal Screening, Reactive & Treated (Pregnant Women)

dqc_rules.message: Syphilis screening coverage exceeds 200% of projected population
dqc_rules.hint: Coverage is measured against a projected population (an estimate), so over 100% can be real. Over 200% almost always means a data error — check the count or the population figure in the source file and re-upload.
Reactive cases are a subset of those screened, so this should never exceed 100%. Check the source file — likely a transcription error.
Only reactive women are eligible for treatment, so this should never exceed 100%. Check the source file — likely a transcription error.

notes: 5 extra columns (14-18, 'treated') vs infec_hiv.json/infec_hepatitisb.json's 14-column layout — confirmed intentional via change_log (syphilis has a curative antenatal treatment protocol HIV/HepB don't). All REACTIVE and TREATED columns are is_sensitive=True (treated implies reactive status, so it carries the same disclosure risk) — confirmed with Joseph 2026-07-09. KNOWN DATA-QUALITY ISSUE (do not silently fix): the source file's Population column (SYPH_POP_U1) is blank/NaN in every sample row across all quarters, while the same column is populated in the HIV/HepB files for the same period/locations — this is specific to the Syphilis file, not a sitewide gap. SYPH_SCREEN_PCT will compute as 0 or null for every row until DOH corrects this; flag to the data owner rather than trusting the percentage. Quarterly upload only; Annual sheet is a pure rollup, not ingested.
